NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI assistant designed for cross-platform persistence and self-improvement through a built-in learning loop. It functions by creating and refining skills from experience, managing long-term memory via periodic nudges, and utilizing a dialectic user modeling system to build a deepening profile of the user. The system supports six execution backends including Docker, SSH, and serverless environments, allowing it to run on hardware ranging from low-cost VPS instances to GPU clusters. Users can interact with the agent through a terminal UI or via messaging integrations like Telegram, Discord, and Slack while switching between over 200 LLM providers.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience
Multi-platform support including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp
Flexible execution backends like Docker, SSH, Modal, and Vercel Sandbox

NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling.summary: This repository provides the implementation and training framework for enabling function calling and structured JSON output in Hermes 2 Pro models. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate, execute, and interpret tool calls based on provided schemas, supporting complex multi-turn interactions. Users can define custom tools via Python functions or enforce specific data structures using Pydantic models. The project includes specialized scripts for financial data retrieval and demonstrates compatibility with the ChatML prompt format.
highlights: Enables recursive function calling and structured JSON mode inference
Supports custom tool definition via Python and Pydantic schemas
Implements ChatML prompt formatting for OpenAI-compatible multi-turn dialogue

NousResearch/atropos.summary: Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning with large language models. It operates through a trajectory API that facilitates data exchange between environment services and external trainers or inference engines. The system supports diverse interactive settings including tool-calling, code execution, and multimodal tasks to guide models toward optimal performance. Notable applications include training models for complex multi-turn interactions and specialized financial prediction tasks.
highlights: Microservice framework for asynchronous LLM reinforcement learning environments
Trajectory API for standardized data collection and batch processing
Supports tool-calling, code execution, and multimodal environment types

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.
highlights: Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter.summary: The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that allows Hermes Agent to function as a managed employee within the Paperclip company ecosystem. It works by wrapping the Hermes CLI to parse raw terminal output into structured transcript entries, enabling Paperclip to render interactive tool cards and status icons. The adapter manages session persistence across heartbeats, allowing agents to maintain context while responding to task assignments or issue comments. It also synchronizes skills between local directories and the Paperclip interface while reclassifying system logs to ensure a clean user experience.
highlights: Integrates 30+ native tools and 80+ skills into Paperclip
Parses raw CLI output into structured markdown and tool cards
Supports eight inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.
highlights: Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

outsourc-e/hermes-workspace.summary: Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to provide a unified graphical interface for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It integrates real-time chat, a cross-platform terminal, and a file browser with specialized tools for managing agent memory, skills, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. The platform features a 'Swarm Mode' that uses persistent tmux-backed workers to orchestrate multiple autonomous agents across role-based dispatch lanes. Users can monitor agent state, track costs, and manage complex missions through a centralized dashboard and Kanban task board.
highlights: Unified interface for chat, terminal, memory editing, and skill management.
Swarm Mode for orchestrating multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents.
Zero-fork compatibility with vanilla Hermes Agent and dashboard installations.

fathah/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native GUI companion for the Hermes Agent that simplifies the installation and management of the self-improving AI assistant. The application automates dependency resolution and environment setup, allowing users to interact with the agent through a streaming chat interface rather than a CLI. It provides centralized control over 14 toolsets, 16 messaging gateways, and complex features like cron-based task scheduling and memory management. Users can run the agent on a local backend or connect to remote API servers while managing multiple isolated profiles and model providers.
highlights: Automated guided installation for local or remote Hermes Agent backends
Management of 16 messaging gateways including Telegram, Discord, and Slack
Integrated tools for memory editing, persona configuration, and task scheduling

sanchomuzax/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a process monitoring and configuration dashboard designed to provide a visual interface for the Hermes Agent. It functions as a non-intrusive polling bridge that reads from the agent's SQLite database and YAML files without requiring modifications to the core agent code. Users can monitor real-time activity feeds, track costs, and inspect full conversation histories through a responsive web interface. The tool also provides read-only access to environment configurations and allows for the management of scheduled cron jobs and agent skills.
highlights: Real-time dashboard for session activity, gateway status, and cost tracking
Full conversation history inspection with FTS5 search and message-level detail
Read-only configuration viewer and management interface for scheduled cron jobs

Euraika-Labs/pan-ui.summary: Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed specifically for the Hermes Agent runtime. It provides a centralized interface to manage agent interactions through a chat-first environment that supports SSE streaming and tool timelines. Users can discover and install skills from skills.sh, manage MCP integrations, and organize work into isolated profiles with unique memory and sessions. The system includes a setup wizard that automatically detects Hermes configurations and can run as a background daemon or system service.
highlights: Chat-first workspace with real-time SSE streaming and tool approval cards
Integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers
Isolated profile management for separate sessions, memory, and API keys

mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.summary: Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is an open-source library of 754 structured skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain-specific knowledge of a senior security analyst. Each skill follows the agentskills.io standard, utilizing YAML frontmatter and structured Markdown to define step-by-step execution workflows and decision-making logic. The library maps every skill across five major frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. It is compatible with over 26 platforms such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Hermes Agent for use cases in threat hunting, digital forensics, and cloud security.
highlights: 754 structured skills across 26 security domains for AI agents.
Unified mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST frameworks.
Follows agentskills.io standard for sub-second discovery and execution.

conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing.summary: Avoid-ai-writing is a portable skill for AI agents designed to audit and rewrite text to eliminate detectable machine-generated patterns. It utilizes a structured two-pass process that identifies 42 specific pattern categories and applies a 109-entry word replacement table across three severity tiers. Users can choose between a full rewrite mode that fixes issues or a detection-only mode for auditing content without altering the source. The project is compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw, providing a standardized way to improve text naturalness across different agentic platforms.
highlights: Identifies 42 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells
Uses a 109-entry tiered word replacement table for plain language
Performs a second-pass audit to catch lingering machine-generated patterns

wondelai/skills.summary: Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.
highlights: Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills.summary: pydantic-ai-skills is a standardized framework for managing modular agent capabilities within the Pydantic AI ecosystem. It implements the Agent Skills open specification by providing a tool-calling mechanism that allows agents to discover and execute specialized scripts or instructions. The library uses a progressive disclosure approach, loading skill details only when relevant to minimize token consumption. Developers can define these skills through local filesystem directories or programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses.
highlights: Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

Agents365-ai/drawio-skill.summary: This project is a diagramming skill that converts natural-language descriptions into professional .drawio XML files and high-resolution image exports. It utilizes the native draw.io desktop CLI to render diagrams and features a self-correction mechanism that analyzes its own output to fix layout issues like overlapping labels or stacked edges. The tool supports six specialized presets including UML, ERD, and machine learning architectures while allowing users to capture and reuse custom visual styles from existing files. It integrates with various AI agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes through the Agent Skills format.
highlights: Generates .drawio XML and exports to PNG, SVG, PDF, or JPG
Self-checks and auto-fixes layout issues using visual feedback loops
Six diagram presets including Architecture, UML, and Deep Learning

smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills.summary: This repository provides a collection of standardized coding skills for AI agents to interact with the Chainlink oracle network and smart contract ecosystem. Built on the agentskills.io specification, these tools allow agents to perform tasks such as managing cross-chain transfers, integrating data feeds, and implementing verifiable randomness. Developers can install specific skills via the Vercel open skills CLI to enable agents to generate workflows, handle on-chain verification, and manage runtime operations across multiple blockchain networks.
highlights: Standardized skills for CCIP, Data Feeds, VRF, and Data Streams
Supports CRE onboarding, workflow generation, and local simulations
Compatible with Vercel's open skills ecosystem CLI for easy installation

tlehman/litprog-skill.summary: The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.
highlights: Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

esaradev/icarus-plugin.summary: Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.
highlights: Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

chigwell/skilldock.io.summary: SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to discover, install, and version AI capabilities. The platform supports a commerce layer for buying and selling skills using Stripe or TON blockchain payments. Developers can package local skill folders, manage dependencies, and publish immutable releases with metadata defined in Markdown frontmatter.
highlights: OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for skill discovery and management
Supports skill monetization via Stripe and TON blockchain payment rails
Automated skill installation with recursive dependency resolution and versioning

black-forest-labs/skills.summary: This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.
highlights: Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory.summary: Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.
highlights: Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

ReinaMacCredy/maestro.summary: Maestro is a local-first agent harness that manages the spec-to-ship lifecycle through a structured on-disk state model. It uses a CLI to maintain shared state in a local directory, allowing separate agent sessions to collaborate without a central database or server. By persisting specs, tasks, and handoff envelopes as files, it ensures agents can resume work with durable continuation records and auditable evidence. The system enforces discipline through machine-checked contracts and a deterministic verification process that gates task completion.
highlights: Maintains shared agent state locally in .maestro/ without a database
Decomposes complex specs into multi-task execution plans and PRs
Emits passive JSON handoff envelopes for seamless cross-session agent transitions

tiann/execplan-skill.summary: ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.
highlights: Autonomous execution for long-running tasks
Real-time tracking of progress and decisions
Built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery

cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill.summary: Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client designed to optimize token consumption through a three-layer lazy loading architecture. It implements a progressive disclosure pattern that fetches server metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when required by the agent. By utilizing a socket-based daemon to manage persistent connections, it avoids the overhead of loading entire toolsets into the AI context at once. This approach is particularly effective for complex MCP servers like Playwright where only a subset of tools is needed for a given task.
highlights: Three-layer progressive disclosure reduces token usage by up to 86%
Socket-based daemon architecture maintains persistent MCP server connections
Supports hot-reloading configurations without restarting the background process

armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.summary: Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discussions into verified instruction files for AI agents. It uses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) analysis and documentation scraping to extract precise function signatures, parameter types, and usage patterns. Every generated instruction includes a provenance map linking back to specific file line numbers at pinned commits or documentation URLs. This approach prevents AI hallucinations by providing agents with version-pinned, immutable skills that comply with the agentskills.io specification.
highlights: Generates verified AI instructions from code repositories and documentation
Provides traceability with direct links to source file line numbers
Supports version-pinned skill management to prevent instruction rot

amanning3390/hermeshub.summary: HermesHub is a curated skills registry for Hermes Agent that provides a secure way to browse, install, and share AI agent capabilities. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard and an automated security scanner that checks for threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration. The platform includes a creator marketplace supporting x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skills, as well as an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scoring.
highlights: Automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules
Creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe-based payment protocols
Agent-to-agent feedback system for reliability and trust scores

PederHP/skillsdotnet.summary: SkillsDotNet is a C#/.NET implementation of the agentskills.io standard designed to integrate agent skills into the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It uses a skill:// URI convention to implement progressive disclosure, allowing clients to discover lightweight skill metadata before loading full documentation or supporting files. The library provides seamless integration with Microsoft.Extensions.AI and supports automatic discovery of skills from various agent directories like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot.
highlights: Implements progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files
Integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based skill loading
Supports automatic registration of common agent skill directories

mem0ai/mem0.summary: Mem0 is a universal memory layer designed to provide AI agents and assistants with long-term context and personalization. It utilizes a multi-signal retrieval system that combines semantic search, BM25 keyword matching, and entity linking to store and recall user preferences and session history. The system features a single-pass extraction algorithm that accumulates memories without overwriting, enabling temporal reasoning for past, present, and future events. It is available as a self-hosted Apache-2.0 library, a Docker-based server, or a managed cloud service.
highlights: Multi-level memory retention for users, sessions, and autonomous agents
Hybrid retrieval fusing semantic, keyword, and entity-based signals
Temporal reasoning for ranking memories by relevant dates and states

vectorize-io/hindsight.summary: Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to enable long-term learning and information retention for autonomous AI agents. It utilizes a workflow of retain, recall, and reflect operations to move beyond simple conversation history and build persistent mental models. The system can be integrated via an LLM wrapper for automatic memory management or through direct SDK calls for granular control. It supports multi-user isolation through metadata filtering and is compatible with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
highlights: Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term learning
Two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory storage and retrieval
Supports PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database for enterprise storage

greyhaven-ai/autocontext.summary: Autocontext is a recursive self-improving harness designed to help AI agents succeed on complex tasks through iterative evaluation. It operates by running a multi-role loop—including competitors, analysts, and coaches—that tests strategies against real-world scenarios and discards ineffective approaches. Successful outcomes are distilled into structured playbooks, datasets, and traces that future agent iterations inherit as persistent knowledge. The system integrates with various providers like Anthropic and OpenAI, and offers specific CLI and MCP support for Hermes Agent and Claude Code.
highlights: Iterative self-improvement loop using five specialized agent roles
Generates structured playbooks and traces for persistent knowledge transfer
Native integration with Hermes Agent via CLI-first skill exports

elkimek/honcho-self-hosted.summary: This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.
highlights: Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

yoloshii/ClawMem.summary: ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine that provides AI agents with persistent, on-device knowledge. It utilizes a hybrid architecture combining multi-signal retrieval, intent classification, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant context from markdown notes and session transcripts. The system integrates with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents via a shared SQLite vault. It enables agents to capture decisions, generate session handoffs, and maintain a self-evolving memory layer without cloud dependencies.
highlights: Local-first architecture with no API keys or cloud dependencies
Hybrid retrieval using BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs
Integrates via MCP server, Claude Code hooks, and agent plugins

plur-ai/plur.summary: PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk and uses a hybrid BM25 and embedding-based retrieval system to inject relevant context into agent prompts. The system employs an ACT-R activation model where facts strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to prevent context clutter. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools, Claude Code, and Hermes Agent to automate the capture and recall of project-specific knowledge.
highlights: Stores memory locally as open-format YAML engrams with zero API costs
Uses ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale ones
Provides MCP tools for automated learning and hybrid context injection

amanning3390/flowstate-qmd.summary: FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes a SQLite-based vector store and an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents by monitoring active agent session logs. This approach allows agents to access design docs, ADRs, and changelogs before initiating a reactive search loop. The system integrates via an MCP server and supports local execution using GGUF models and node-llama-cpp.
highlights: Anticipatory cache prefetches context by watching agent session logs
Local-first storage using SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp
MCP-native server with automated configuration for major coding agent clients

robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus.summary: Web-search-plus is a multi-provider search and URL extraction plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It uses a capability-based routing engine called Routing v2 to automatically select the best available search provider based on the user's configured API keys. The tool supports over a dozen backends including Serper, Tavily, and Exa, while offering clean content extraction through dedicated providers like Firecrawl and Linkup. It features a standalone setup wizard with presets to help users manage multiple API keys and configure provider priorities without manual environment file editing.
highlights: Adds web_search_plus and web_extract_plus tools to Hermes Agent
Auto-routes queries across Serper, Tavily, Exa, and You.com
Supports clean URL extraction via Linkup, Firecrawl, and Parallel

42-evey/hermes-plugins.summary: Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.
highlights: Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin.summary: The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.
highlights: Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin.summary: The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.
highlights: Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

nativ3ai/hermes-payguard.summary: Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.
highlights: Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare.summary: This plugin integrates Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable web crawling and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy websites. It utilizes Cloudflare's headless browser infrastructure to perform tasks like CSS-selector scraping, PDF generation, and full-page screenshot capture. The tool also features AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3 70B via Workers AI. Users can automate complex web workflows including sitemap discovery and Markdown conversion directly through agent commands.
highlights: Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
Converts fully rendered JavaScript-heavy pages into clean Markdown

builderz-labs/mission-control.summary: Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to orchestrate and monitor fleets of AI agents. It utilizes a local-first architecture powered by SQLite and Next.js to coordinate multi-agent workflows, dispatch tasks, and track operational costs without external database dependencies. The platform features built-in quality gates for task approval, real-time telemetry via WebSockets, and a security framework that includes secret detection and trust scoring. It integrates with various agent frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph, and provides a bridge for CLI tools such as Claude Code.
highlights: Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, and Claude SDK
Built-in Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off

swarmclawai/swarmclaw.summary: SwarmClaw is a self-hosted AI agent runtime and multi-agent framework designed to orchestrate autonomous swarms. It utilizes a centralized dashboard to manage agent memory, runtime skills, and delegation across more than 23 LLM providers. The platform supports durable conversation history and a unique learning mechanism that converts reviewed interactions into reusable skills. Users can deploy it as a desktop application, via Docker, or as a global npm package to create complex organizational charts of interacting agents.
highlights: Supports 23+ LLM providers including Ollama, OpenAI, and Anthropic.
Features durable agent memory and conversation-to-skill learning capabilities.
Orchestrates multi-agent swarms with delegation, heartbeats, and scheduling.

howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness.summary: Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system orchestrates benchmark evaluations, tracks performance frontiers, and performs structured candidate mutations to find superior harness variants. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks through a research-safe workflow that emphasizes archive analysis and deterministic search.
highlights: Optimizes agent harness code through automated outer-loop search
Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations via Hermes
Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

runtimenoteslabs/gladiator.summary: Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.
highlights: Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent.summary: Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system built for OpenCode AI to manage complex software development lifecycles. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route tasks through specialized pipelines involving 17 distinct agents focused on research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure. The system enforces mandatory quality gates, such as automated reviews and testing, while managing context flow between agents to ensure consistency. It integrates with MCP servers for external documentation retrieval and supports specific workflows for bug fixing, refactoring, and security-sensitive features.
highlights: Coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains
Enforces mandatory quality and security gates for code changes
Automates task routing through predefined research and implementation pipelines

supermodeltools/bigiron.summary: Big Iron is an experimental AI-native software development system that automates the full development lifecycle through a graph-gated workflow. It integrates Hermes Agent with the Supermodel Code Graph API to replace traditional file-loading context with structural intelligence, including call graphs and dependency maps. The system executes an eight-phase process—from planning to autonomous health checks—using specific skills and quality gates to ensure architectural alignment. While now deprecated in favor of the Supermodel CLI, it serves as a prototype for autonomous, graph-grounded code generation and refactoring.
highlights: Executes an autonomous eight-phase SDLC from planning to health checks
Uses Supermodel MCP to provide Hermes with structural code graphs
Enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff phase gates

Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill.summary: The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.
highlights: Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

numtide/llm-agents.nix.summary: llm-agents.nix is a collection of Nix packages designed to simplify the deployment and management of AI coding agents and development tools. The project provides a centralized repository that automatically updates daily, offering reproducible builds and easy execution via Nix flakes. Users can run a wide variety of terminal-based agents, multi-agent orchestrators, and API proxies including tools like Claude Code, Goose, and Bernstein. This infrastructure allows developers to integrate diverse agentic workflows into their local environments without manual installation or dependency conflicts.
highlights: Provides daily updated Nix packages for AI coding agents
Supports multi-agent orchestrators and unified API proxies
Enables reproducible execution of terminal-based AI development tools

TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within NVIDIA’s OpenShell sandbox to provide hardware-level security enforcement. It utilizes kernel-level mechanisms like Landlock LSM and Seccomp BPF to restrict the agent's filesystem access, network egress, and system calls. Users can toggle between strict and permissive policy presets to enable features like web searching or messaging integrations while maintaining an out-of-process security boundary. The system supports persistent memory, self-improving skills, and multi-platform communication via Telegram and Discord.
highlights: Hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking
Three security policy presets ranging from strict inference to permissive web-access
Pre-built skills library for research digests, code reviews, and market alerts

0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent.summary: nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.
highlights: Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows-native desktop application that provides a user-friendly interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It operates through a Tkinter-based GUI or CLI, utilizing an embedded Python environment to connect with local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The agent features a library of 100 tools for tasks including music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation. Users can also build custom automation pipelines through a built-in workflow engine and a dynamic tool maker for wrapping REST APIs.
highlights: Zero-install Windows agent with 100 built-in tools across 20 toolsets
Local model support via LM Studio and portable AI generation servers
Dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for custom automation pipelines

xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker.summary: This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.
highlights: Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
Supports persistent state via mounted home directories
Allows building specific Hermes tags using build arguments

JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server.summary: This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.
highlights: Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes.summary: This project provides a Docker Compose configuration for deploying the Hermes agent alongside a ttyd web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to facilitate browser-based terminal access to the agent. Users can manage the deployment as a Git-based stack within the Portainer UI for simplified container orchestration. The setup includes an automated GitHub Action workflow for building and updating the underlying container image.
highlights: Deploys Hermes agent with a ttyd web terminal interface
Integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack
Automates image builds using GitHub Actions and GHCR

raulvidis/hermes-android.summary: Hermes-android is a remote device control bridge that enables AI agents to interact with Android hardware and software. It establishes a WebSocket connection between a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset on the server, allowing commands to bypass NAT and firewalls without port forwarding. The system provides 38 specialized tools for UI manipulation, media control, and system functions like SMS or GPS. It is also compatible with Android Automotive OS, extending agent capabilities to vehicle head units.
highlights: Provides 38 tools for UI interaction, navigation, and system automation
Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and remote input execution
Connects via WebSocket using a simple 6-character pairing code

teknium1/hermes-miniverse.summary: Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council.summary: Hermes Council is an MCP server that provides an adversarial judgment layer for Hermes Agent to stress-test plans and actions before execution. It utilizes a multi-perspective deliberation process involving personas like Advocate, Skeptic, and Arbiter to generate structured verdicts on code diffs, claims, and risky commands. This approach helps prevent overconfident agent failures by producing explicit signals such as blocking risks, required checks, and DPO preference pairs for reinforcement learning workflows.
highlights: Multi-persona adversarial deliberation using Advocate, Skeptic, and Oracle roles
Structured safety gating with allow, deny, and conditional verdicts
Generates DPO preference pairs and rewards for RL training workflows

marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors.summary: Zouroboros Swarm Executors is a bridge system that integrates local CLI-based AI tools into the Zo Computer swarm orchestration ecosystem. It functions by using bash bridge scripts to wrap tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, allowing them to receive tasks via standard input and return results via standard output. This approach enables local execution of complex tasks such as security audits and web research while maintaining a shared identity and memory system across all personas. The project includes a health-check system to validate environment configurations and a registry for adding custom CLI tools as swarm members.
highlights: Wraps CLI tools into AI personas with shared identity files
Provides bridge scripts for Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex
Routes tasks locally instead of through remote API calls

gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle.summary: Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.
highlights: Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

junhoyeo/tokscale.summary: Tokscale is a high-performance CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It works by scanning local data directories, databases, and session files from supported clients to aggregate usage statistics into a native Rust TUI or a web-based dashboard. The tool provides detailed daily summaries, model-specific breakdowns, and a 3D contribution graph for visualizing activity. It supports a wide range of integrations including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and GitHub Copilot CLI.
highlights: Tracks token usage and costs across multiple AI coding agents
Native Rust TUI for high-performance local data visualization
Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Gemini, and Cursor IDE

joeynyc/hermes-skins.summary: Hermes Skins is a collection of community-contributed visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent. The project uses YAML configuration files to customize terminal aesthetics including banner colors, ASCII art, spinner animations, and branding text without altering the agent's underlying logic. Users can apply themes for a session or permanently by placing files in a local directory, with missing values automatically inheriting from a default template. Available styles range from retro computing and cyberpunk aesthetics to minimalist monochrome and anime-inspired designs.
highlights: Customizes CLI visuals including ASCII banners and spinner animations
Uses YAML files for easy theme configuration and sharing
Inherits from default skin for partial configuration support

AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC.summary: hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.
highlights: Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser.summary: Vessel is an open-source, Chromium-based browser designed specifically for autonomous agent control rather than traditional human-centric browsing. It provides a persistent runtime with durable state and a visible interface that allows humans to audit, intervene, and redirect long-running agent workflows. The browser integrates with external agent harnesses via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and includes built-in tools for page schema inference, action undo/rollback, and agent-meaningful bookmarks. It is optimized for persistent web agents that require a real browser environment instead of a headless, stateless execution model.
highlights: Provides a human-visible UI for auditing and supervising autonomous agents
Integrates with MCP clients like Hermes Agent for external control
Maintains durable state with named sessions, checkpoints, and action rollback

Abruptive/Ankh.md.summary: Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.
highlights: Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

roli-lpci/lintlang.summary: lintlang is a static analysis tool designed to identify structural and linguistic failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool definitions. It utilizes regex and AST-based heuristics rather than LLM calls to detect issues like vague tool descriptions and missing termination conditions. This approach ensures deterministic, zero-cost scans that run in approximately 2ms per file. The tool is intended for use in CI/CD pipelines to prevent prompt drift and logic errors before runtime.
highlights: Deterministic structural analysis using 7 specific detector patterns (H1–H7)
Zero LLM calls for cost-effective and offline CI integration
Supports YAML, JSON, plain text, and .prompt file formats

0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes.summary: openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.
highlights: Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

42-evey/evey-setup.summary: Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.
highlights: Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

ucsandman/DashClaw.summary: DashClaw is a governance and decision infrastructure layer designed to secure AI agents interacting with real-world systems. It intercepts risky agent actions to evaluate them against declarative policies, routing high-risk tasks for human approval via a dashboard or mobile interface. The system records verifiable evidence for every decision and tracks terminal outcomes to prevent double-execution during agent retries. It integrates with various runtimes including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and LangChain through MCP servers, SDKs, or lifecycle hooks.
highlights: Evaluates risky agent actions against declarative guard policies
Routes pending approvals to CLI, mobile, or messaging platforms
Prevents double-execution by tracking durable terminal outcomes

Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander.summary: Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and apply fixes across CPU, memory, disk, and service layers. Following resolution, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and automatically creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and uses cron scheduling for continuous health audits.
highlights: Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports automatically.
Integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time P0 alerts.

Christabel337/job-scout-agent.summary: Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.
highlights: Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

rodmarkun/anihermes.summary: AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.
highlights: Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-moni.summary: This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous, long-running monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured markdown reports covering compute risk analysis, cost projections, and trend dashboards. It serves as a template for developers to deploy headless, scheduled agents that transform raw research data into actionable infrastructure intelligence.
highlights: Automated research ingestion via arXiv API and GitHub ecosystem tracking
Scheduled cron workflows for risk analysis and cost modeling
Generates terminal-style dashboards and structured markdown intelligence reports

bigph00t/hermescraft.summary: HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.
highlights: Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

hxsteric/mercury.summary: Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.
highlights: Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

bryercowan/hermes-embodied.summary: Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.
highlights: Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent.summary: This project is a curated directory of resources designed to expand the capabilities and workflows of the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes community-contributed skills, tools, integrations, and documentation into categorized lists for easier discovery. Users can find everything from deployment utilities and messaging bridges to specialized domain applications and multi-agent orchestration tools. The repository also includes maturity tags to help developers distinguish between stable production tools and experimental proofs of concept.
highlights: Curated list of community skills and plugins
Categorized resources for deployment and integrations
Maturity tags for assessing tool stability

alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book.summary: The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.
highlights: Detailed guides on memory systems and learning loops
Step-by-step setup and multi-platform customization instructions
Practical scenarios for dev automation and multi-agent systems

OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide.summary: The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive documentation and resource repository designed to help users deploy and scale Hermes Agent in production environments. It provides a 24-part instructional series alongside runnable artifacts like VPS bootstrap scripts, opinionated configuration templates, and pre-configured skills. The project enables users to implement advanced features such as multi-platform orchestration, MCP server integration, and cost-optimized model routing across 22+ communication channels. By utilizing reference architectures and security playbooks, it bridges the gap between a basic installation and a hardened, observable agent deployment.
highlights: One-command VPS bootstrap script for automated production-ready deployment.
Thirteen installable skills and five opinionated configuration templates.
Cost-optimized routing across 12 models with context compression strategies.

metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu.summary: This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation scripts for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2 or natively on macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with the Qwen3.5 model family, utilizing GPU acceleration through CUDA or Metal. The guide covers the entire stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. By following this documentation, users can deploy a private, local agentic environment capable of browser automation and LLM interaction.
highlights: Automated installation scripts for Ubuntu (WSL2) and macOS platforms
Configures llama.cpp with CUDA or Metal for GPU-accelerated inference
Integrates Camofox browser server for autonomous web-browsing capabilities

mudrii/hermes-agent-docs.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform task execution. It utilizes a closed-loop architecture that creates and refines skills from experience while maintaining long-term memory via SQLite and FTS5 session search. The agent operates across diverse environments including a messaging gateway for over 20 platforms, an Ink-based TUI, and an OpenAI-compatible API server. Its plugin architecture allows for modular extension of tools, providers, and transport layers without modifying the core codebase.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience
Unified messaging gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp
Pluggable transport layer for Anthropic, Bedrock, OpenAI, and local providers

martymcenroe/HermesWiki.summary: HermesWiki is a dedicated documentation repository that serves as a central knowledge base for the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides users with structured guidance on how to implement and manage the agent's email automation capabilities. The wiki contains deployment patterns and practical recipes to assist developers in configuring the system for various environments. By consolidating these resources, the project helps users navigate the setup process and optimize their autonomous email workflows.
highlights: Centralized documentation for the Hermes autonomous AI email agent
Provides specific deployment patterns for various environments
Includes practical recipes for configuring email automation workflows

nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel.summary: This project is a version of the Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL-style trust boundaries to protect against indirect prompt injection. It implements an opt-in runtime guard that monitors or enforces security policies by gating sensitive tool side effects against verified user intent. The system maintains the core Hermes features, including a self-improving learning loop, autonomous skill creation, and cross-platform accessibility via Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Users can toggle between monitoring modes to record policy decisions or enforcement modes to block unauthorized actions during autonomous execution.
highlights: Opt-in CaMeL runtime guard for indirect prompt-injection protection
Self-improving learning loop with autonomous skill creation and memory
Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and Signal

kaminocorp/hermes-alpha.summary: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities of the Hermes model. The system integrates with Fly.io for managed infrastructure and uses Elephantasm for long-term memory streams.
highlights: Autonomous two-agent loop for self-improving vulnerability research
Web-based terminal interface with persistent WebSocket sessions
Managed deployment of sub-agents via Fly.io infrastructure

beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation.summary: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from a diverse 30-task battery and converts them into SFT-ready data or live RL signals for the Atropos training framework. The system uses a multi-dimensional reward function to score trajectories based on task completion, efficiency, and error recovery. This approach enables a closed learning loop where production-style agent interactions serve as the primary training data for future model iterations.
highlights: Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from real-world tool usage trajectories
Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning via GRPO
Automated scoring based on completion, efficiency, and error recovery

xaspx/hermes-control-interface.summary: The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It provides a multi-agent gateway to start, stop, and configure various agent profiles while monitoring real-time system metrics and token analytics. Users can interact with agents via a revamped chat interface featuring tool-call visualization, manage scheduled tasks through a cron manager, and edit files using a secure browser-based explorer. The platform includes robust security features like role-based access control, CSRF protection, and encrypted authentication.
highlights: Multi-agent gateway for managing and configuring several Hermes profiles
Real-time chat interface with tool-call cards and session resumption
Comprehensive token analytics tracking costs, models, and platform usage

ksimback/hermes-ecosystem.summary: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.
highlights: Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

dodo-reach/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a focused workbench for managing Hermes Agent hosts. It establishes a direct connection over SSH to interact with remote sessions, files, and workflows without requiring a browser or additional daemon. The app maintains the remote host as the single source of truth, allowing users to manage multiple agent profiles, edit skills, and monitor usage. It also features an embedded terminal and local workflow presets to streamline agent interactions on Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
highlights: Connects directly to Hermes hosts via native SSH and aliases
Manages remote sessions, Kanban boards, and cron jobs natively
Supports multi-agent workflows through selectable Hermes profiles

clawshell/clawshell.summary: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.
highlights: Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

nesquena/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI provides a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, offering a graphical alternative to the standard command-line environment. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, the application requires no build steps or frameworks and maintains full feature parity with the CLI. The interface features a three-panel layout for session management, chat, and workspace file browsing, including a visual context ring for monitoring token usage. It allows users to manage persistent memory, scheduled cron jobs, and self-improving skills through a secure web portal accessible via mobile or desktop.
highlights: Three-panel layout for chat, sessions, and workspace file browsing
Full feature parity with Hermes CLI using vanilla JavaScript
Visual context ring for real-time token usage monitoring

garrytan/gbrain.summary: GBrain is an autonomous memory and synthesis layer designed to serve as the institutional or personal knowledge base for AI agents. It combines a self-wiring knowledge graph with a synthesis engine to provide cited prose answers and gap analysis rather than simple document retrieval. The system automatically extracts entity relationships and runs background processes to consolidate memory from diverse sources like meetings, emails, and notes. It supports multi-user environments with scoped data access and integrates directly with Hermes and OpenClaw via the Model Context Protocol.
highlights: Synthesizes cited prose answers across people, companies, and ideas.
Self-wiring knowledge graph extracts entity relationships without LLM calls.
Automated background cycles for memory consolidation and data enrichment.

pyrate-llama/hermes-ui.summary: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface that serves as a centralized command center for managing self-hosted Hermes Agents. It operates as a single-file React application connected to a lightweight Python proxy server to facilitate real-time communication and system monitoring. Users can interact through a multi-panel dashboard featuring chat, live log streaming, file browsing, and memory inspection. The interface also supports specialized workflows including cron job scheduling, MCP tool management, and workspace-based video uploads.
highlights: Single-file React 18 application with a lightweight Python proxy server
Integrated file browser, terminal, and real-time log streaming interface
Management of MCP tools, skill browsing, and scheduled cron jobs

kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.
highlights: Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

AaronWong1999/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI brains or activate multiple agents at once using simple chat commands. It also introduces WeChat support for OpenCode, enabling voice-based coding tasks and access to free models like MiniMax M2.5.
highlights: Runs three AI agents simultaneously on one WeChat account
Prevents iLink token conflicts via local proxy routing
Enables agent switching with /hermes, /openclaw, and /opencode commands

stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm.summary: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that compacts older messages into depth-aware summary nodes. The system persists raw message history in a local SQLite store and provides the agent with specialized tools to drill back into specific compacted details. This approach allows the agent to maintain a bounded active context window while retaining the ability to retrieve exact historical data through source-aware lineage tracking.
highlights: Uses a hierarchical DAG to compact context without losing data
Provides agent tools for grepping and expanding summarized history
Preserves raw messages in a local SQLite database for retrieval

Codename-11/hermes-relay.summary: Hermes-Relay is a dual-surface companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent from Android and desktop environments. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay and a server-side plugin to facilitate real-time communication, terminal access, and device control without requiring SSH. The Android app allows the agent to read screens and perform actions like tapping or swiping, while the desktop CLI and tray app provide local file access, terminal execution, and clipboard integration for the remote agent. This setup allows users to leverage powerful remote compute while maintaining the experience of a locally running agent on their personal devices.
highlights: Native Android app for remote chat, voice, and screen-based device control.
Desktop CLI and Windows tray app for local-style terminal interaction.
WSS relay enables agent access to local files and system clipboards.

clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp.summary: The Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal-style interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within Telegram. It functions as a mobile-first dashboard that connects to a local Hermes installation via a secure gateway, providing a suite of tools for agent control and system monitoring. The application features a streaming chat interface with vision and OCR support, a cron job manager for scheduled tasks, and a status tab for real-time hardware resource tracking. Security is handled through dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure only authorized Telegram users can access the agent.
highlights: Terminal-style chat with streaming responses and slash command support
Background agent spawning with live output monitoring and interactive modes
Integrated management for creating, pausing, and triggering scheduled cron jobs

briancaffey/hermes-otel.summary: hermes-otel is an OpenTelemetry plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables observability by exporting execution data to OTLP-compatible backends. It automatically captures and traces LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as spans to provide visibility into agentic workflows. The plugin supports a wide range of backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Jaeger for monitoring traces, metrics, and logs. Users can configure multiple backends simultaneously via a YAML configuration file to fan out telemetry data in parallel.
highlights: Exports LLM tool calls and model invocations as OTel spans
Supports multiple backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, and SigNoz
Provides automated token aggregation and session I/O roll-up

EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui.summary: Hermes Web UI is a full-featured dashboard designed to manage and monitor Hermes Agent instances through a responsive web interface.
