It facilitates real-time AI chat streaming via Socket.IO and provides a unified configuration hub for eight communication platforms, including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. The system includes local SQLite session management, usage analytics for cost tracking, and a multi-profile architecture for isolated agent configurations. Users can also manage scheduled cron jobs, browse remote files, and access an integrated web terminal for direct system interaction.
highlights: Unified configuration for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp channels
Real-time chat streaming with multi-session management and Markdown support
Usage analytics for tracking token consumption and estimated costs

Web3CZ/Web3Hermes.summary: Web3Hermes is a lightweight WebUI for Hermes Agent specifically localized for Chinese-speaking users. It provides a desktop-optimized interface that simplifies interaction with the agent through a localized workflow and full Chinese language support. The platform integrates a built-in workspace file browser, session management tools, and support for multiple AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. Users can manage tasks, edit code with syntax highlighting, and visualize tool calls within a responsive dark or light themed environment.
highlights: Localized Chinese interface with dark and light theme support
Integrated workspace file browser for direct file editing and management
Comprehensive session management including export, search, and tool call visualization

AxDSan/mnemosyne.summary: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed for the Hermes Agent framework to provide persistent storage for conversations and knowledge without cloud dependencies. It utilizes an SQLite backend with native vector search and full-text search to enable sub-millisecond retrieval and storage. The system features the MEMORIA Fact Engine for structured fact extraction and recursive gap analysis to support multi-hop reasoning. It offers a fully private, offline-capable alternative to cloud-based memory providers with built-in tools for importing data from other platforms.
highlights: Local-first SQLite storage with native vector and full-text search
Sub-millisecond latency for memory read, write, and search operations
MEMORIA engine for structured fact extraction and temporal reasoning

keepnotes-ai/keep.summary: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that facilitates long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media—by summarizing, embedding, and indexing them into a searchable graph. The system uses 'edge tags' to create navigable relationships between notes, such as linking authors to their specific contributions. It integrates with various agent frameworks via MCP and provides structured prompts to help agents reflect on past actions and current intentions.
highlights: Semantic search with BM25 full-text and ranked graph traversal
Automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia files
Edge tags create navigable relationships with automatic inverse links

AMAP-ML/SkillClaw.summary: SkillClaw is an agentic skill management system that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It operates as a background process that automatically deduplicates, merges, and improves skill libraries based on session logs across multiple agents and devices. The system creates a unified evolution loop where experience from different environments—such as home, school, or work—compounds into a single shared library. It natively integrates with Hermes, Claude Code, and various OpenAI-compatible agents to facilitate collective skill growth within teams.
highlights: Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement from interaction logs
Unifies skill libraries across multiple agents, devices, and users
Natively integrates with Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenAI-compatible APIs

aivrar/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring system-wide installations or administrative rights. It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API. The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine for multi-step automation. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrapping and Python scripting.
highlights: Zero-install Windows desktop GUI with granular permission controls
Over 100 tools including TTS, MusicGen, and ComfyUI integrations
Dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers and Python handlers

iamagenius00/hermes-a2a.summary: hermes-a2a is a plugin for Hermes Agent that implements Google's A2A protocol to enable direct peer-to-peer communication between autonomous agents. It works by injecting external messages into an agent's active session via HMAC-signed webhooks, allowing for instant responses without polling or session cloning. This architecture ensures agents maintain a single shared memory with their user while collaborating on tasks like debugging, status reporting, or peer consultation. The system includes built-in security features such as prompt injection filtering, outbound redaction, and independent conversation storage to prevent data loss during context compaction.
highlights: Enables peer-to-peer agent communication without manual user relaying
Injects messages into active sessions to maintain consistent agent memory
Triggers instant agent responses via HMAC-signed webhook architecture

Cranot/super-hermes.summary: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.
highlights: Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

EfficientContext/ContextPilot.summary: ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing KV cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping context blocks into shared prefixes before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant token computation and prefill latency without compromising model reasoning quality. The project provides native integrations for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, vLLM, and SGLang, supporting both local deployments and cloud APIs.
highlights: Increases cache hits by 4–12× through prefix reordering and deduplication
Reduces prefill latency by 1.5–3× across RAG and agentic workloads
Native plugin support for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw ecosystems

335234131/agent-browser-mcp.summary: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.
highlights: Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies
Executes native CDP commands for advanced DOM and browser control
Supports physical mouse and keyboard inputs for human-like automation

jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide.summary: The Hermes Agent White Paper is a comprehensive Chinese-language technical guide designed to bridge the documentation gap for the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book curriculum covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory system, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details practical implementations including MCP protocol automation, multi-platform deployment on WeChat and Discord, and migration paths from OpenClaw. Users can follow specialized reading tracks for rapid deployment, deep technical development, or commercial application scenarios.
highlights: 16-book systematic guide with over 300,000 words of technical content
Deep analysis of five-layer architecture and three-tier memory systems
Step-by-step integration for 15+ platforms and 6,000+ MCP services

longyunfeigu/learn-hermes-agent.summary: Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementations for each stage to help users understand the design backbone of autonomous systems.
highlights: Step-by-step implementation of agent loops and tool dispatch systems
Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WeChat
Advanced memory management with context compression and session persistence

jpalmae/hermeshq.summary: HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to external AI clients like Claude Code and Codex.
highlights: Orchestrates multiple Hermes Agent instances with separate workspaces and identities
Provides hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation and task scheduling
Offers enterprise MCP access for external AI client integrations

plastic-labs/honcho.summary: Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to build stateful agents that maintain context across users, groups, and projects over time. It functions by storing conversation logs and events, then using background reasoning processes to extract conclusions and update persistent peer representations. Developers can query these representations for natural-language insights or retrieve prompt-ready context for injection into LLM calls. The system supports hybrid search and is available as a managed service or a self-hosted FastAPI server with SDKs for Python and TypeScript.
highlights: Extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations instead of simple vector matching
Maintains evolving representations for users, agents, and specific project entities
Provides prompt-ready context compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic message formats

adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud.summary: This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and storing credentials in a restricted local environment file.
highlights: Manages files, notes, calendars, tasks, and contacts via standard protocols.
Communicates securely over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.
Includes a guided setup script for credential validation and configuration.

Xquik-dev/hermes-tweet.summary: Hermes Tweet is a native plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that enables automated interaction with X (formerly Twitter) via the Xquik API. It integrates 99 OpenAPI-generated endpoints into the agent's environment, allowing for structured social media tasks like searching, posting, and trend monitoring. The plugin utilizes a tiered security model that separates read-only operations from account actions, requiring explicit environment configuration to enable write-access tools. It includes a bundled Hermes skill to provide agents with contextual guidance on how to navigate the available toolset.
highlights: Provides 99 agent-callable Xquik endpoints for comprehensive X automation
Separates read and action tools for least-privilege security enforcement
Includes slash commands for quick account status and trend checks

Context4GPTs/klodi-plugin.summary: Klodi is an agent-to-agent marketplace plugin that enables AI agents to list, negotiate, and purchase items on behalf of their users. The system functions through a peer-to-peer architecture where agents interact via structured offers and private negotiation channels while keeping floor prices and strategies stored locally. This approach allows users to delegate the entire lifecycle of a transaction—from initial listing to final sign-off—without exposing private bargaining limits to the marketplace. It provides cross-platform compatibility with adapters for Hermes, OpenClaw, and various Rust-based agent hosts.
highlights: Automated agent-led negotiation for buying and selling items
Local policy files keep floor prices and strategies private
Cross-platform adapters for Hermes, OpenClaw, and Rust hosts

yepyhun/Brainstack.summary: Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to replace flat memory blobs in Hermes agents with a structured, multi-layered memory model. It integrates three distinct memory lines—Hindsight, Graphiti, and MemPalace—to handle session continuity, temporal graph relations, and large-corpus retrieval through a single memory owner. By categorizing information into specific "shelves" like profile, graph, and corpus, it prevents prompt stuffing and ensures Hermes receives only the most relevant, bounded evidence for each query. The system includes a dedicated installer and diagnostic tools to maintain stateful conversation continuity within the Hermes-Agent runtime.
highlights: Consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval
Uses SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma for multi-layered data storage
Provides bounded evidence packing to reduce prompt noise

kfa-ai/hermes-timetree-sync.summary: hermes-timetree-sync is a Python-based CLI and library that enables Hermes Agents to manage TimeTree calendar events without manual user intervention. Since TimeTree discontinued its official API, this bridge uses authenticated web endpoints and session cookies to perform non-interactive writes. It supports creating all-day events directly via HTTP requests and timed events through a Safari-backed automation path. The tool allows agents to translate natural language requests into structured calendar updates using locally stored credentials and optional label mapping policies.
highlights: Syncs all-day and timed events using TimeTree web endpoints
Supports non-interactive batch writes for multiple calendar entries
Maps natural language terms to calendar labels via YAML policy

jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP.summary: MeiGen AI Design MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that integrates professional image and video generation into AI coding environments. It connects to nine leading models, including Midjourney and Flux, through three backend modes: MeiGen cloud, OpenAI-compatible APIs, or local ComfyUI instances. The system features a library of over 1,400 curated prompt templates and supports parallel sub-agent orchestration for batch processing. It is designed for use in tools like Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Cursor to facilitate design tasks like logo creation and product rendering.
highlights: Supports 9 image and video models including local ComfyUI integration
Includes 1,446 curated prompt templates for style-aware generation
Orchestrates parallel batch generation via sub-agents for complex design tasks

alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer.summary: Hermes Tool Slimmer is a context optimization plugin that reduces prompt overhead by dynamically selecting the most relevant tools for each agent turn. It builds an indexable corpus from tool schemas and uses BM25 ranking with optional keyword boosting to filter large toolsets down to a concise, relevant subset. This approach significantly lowers token consumption in environments with dozens of native or MCP tools while maintaining access to essential safety and file-system utilities. The project includes a dashboard for monitoring estimated token savings and provides a fail-open mechanism to ensure agent reliability if selection errors occur.
highlights: Reduces schema overhead using BM25 ranking and keyword-based tool selection
Supports native Hermes tools and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations
Provides a dashboard plugin for tracking estimated prompt token savings

BORT-AGENTS/hermes-bort.summary: hermes-bort is a plugin for Hermes Agent that enables interaction with BORT (BAP-578) agent NFTs on the BSC mainnet. It allows Hermes processes to read on-chain agent states, execute actions via operator-signed writes, and manage portable session memory anchored to IPFS. The plugin integrates with the hermes-agent-self-evolution optimizer to commit verifiable on-chain learning records and evolved skills directly to an agent's knowledge registry.
highlights: Reads full agent state, IPFS identity, and knowledge registries
Executes on-chain actions via VaultPermissionManagerV2 with local policy gating
Anchors local session memory to IPFS and KnowledgeRegistryV2

volcengine/OpenViking.summary: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed to manage the fragmented memory, resources, and skills required by AI agents. It utilizes a filesystem paradigm to organize agent data, replacing traditional flat vector storage with a structured directory-based retrieval system. The platform implements a tiered L0/L1/L2 loading architecture to optimize token usage and provides visual trajectories for debugging retrieval logic. As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, it automates session management by extracting long-term memories from ongoing interactions.
highlights: Unified context management using a hierarchical filesystem paradigm
Tiered L0/L1/L2 loading to reduce token consumption
Recursive directory retrieval combining semantic search with structural positioning

supermemoryai/supermemory.summary: Supermemory is a memory and context engine that provides a persistent information layer for AI agents and applications. It functions by automatically extracting facts from conversations, maintaining dynamic user profiles, and managing knowledge updates or contradictions through a custom vector graph engine. The system integrates RAG, file processing, and real-time connectors into a single API to deliver relevant context with sub-300ms recall. It supports various clients via an MCP server and offers dedicated plugins for tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Cursor.
highlights: Automated fact extraction and user profile maintenance from conversations.
Sub-300ms recall with byte-level deduplication and prompt-cache discounts.
Native connectors for Google Drive, Notion, GitHub, and Gmail.

campfirein/byterover-cli.summary: ByteRover CLI is a persistent memory provider for AI coding agents that structures project knowledge into a version-controlled context tree. It utilizes a 5-tier retrieval system and a git-like workflow—including branching, committing, and merging—to manage markdown-based memory. The tool integrates with over 20 LLM providers and 22 AI agents, offering a REPL interface and a web dashboard for curating codebase information. It supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and provides cloud synchronization for team collaboration and cross-device access.
highlights: Git-like version control for context including branching and merging
Sub-100ms 5-tier retrieval system for long-term conversational memory
Built-in agent tools for file operations and code execution

yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin.summary: yantrikdb-hermes-plugin is a self-maintaining memory provider for Hermes Agent that prevents context loss during long sessions. It utilizes a temporal context graph to manage memory lifecycles through canonicalization, contradiction tracking, and recency-aware ranking. The plugin features explainable retrieval by attaching a why_retrieved tag to every recalled memory and includes a native outcome ledger for agent-authored skills. It operates in an embedded, in-process mode by default, requiring no external server or GPU for deployment.
highlights: Surfaces contradictions and canonicalizes duplicate memories automatically.
Provides explainable recall with why_retrieved tags for every result.
Supports agent-authored skills with a native outcome ledger.

Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin.summary: Hermes Ladybug Memory is a local-first memory provider plugin that enables Hermes agents to store and retrieve information without relying on cloud services or external APIs. It utilizes a columnar embedded graph database to manage typed memory entries, named relationships, and importance-weighted recall. The system prioritizes high-value information using a 1-10 importance scale and supports BM25 keyword search for precise retrieval. Users can also enable optional entity extraction via GLiNER2 to build structured knowledge graphs directly on their local machine.
highlights: Local-only storage using a single .lbdb embedded graph database file
Importance-weighted recall surfaces high-priority memories during context prefetch
Supports BM25 keyword search and named graph relationships between entries

MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory.summary: hermes-agentmemory is a standalone memory plugin for Hermes Agent that ensures episodic memory deletions are complete and immediate. It utilizes a pull-model approach with synchronous writes and on-demand summarization via Claude models, avoiding the persistent derived summaries found in background-consolidation backends. The plugin provides a transparent audit trail of all memory injections through a JSONL trace log. It is designed for users who require reversible growth and auditable memory operations.
highlights: Immediate, complete deletion of episodic memories without derived artifacts
Detailed audit logs of every prefetch in trace.jsonl
On-demand summarization using the Claude model family
